decisions.briefing: Markets are in risk-off mode today with the NASDAQ dropping 1.8% and S&P 500 down 0.9%. The screener returned no candidates, which is the correct signal to sit on hands. With 31% overall P&L to protect and six open positions already running, holding cash and letting stop-losses do their job is the right call today.

market_education.summary_en: Today's risk-off session reflected broad investor caution, with tech-heavy NASDAQ bearing the brunt of selling pressure at -1.8% versus the S&P 500's -0.9% decline. Risk-off days typically occur when macro uncertainty rises — whether from economic data surprises, geopolitical tension, or Federal Reserve commentary — prompting investors to rotate out of growth stocks and into safer assets like bonds, gold, or cash. The wider gap between NASDAQ and S&P 500 losses signals that high-multiple tech names, which are most sensitive to rate expectations and risk appetite, faced the heaviest selling today.
market_education.summary_zh: 今日市場呈現避險情緒，科技股為主的納斯達克指數跌幅達1.8%，標普500指數則下跌0.9%，兩者差距反映出投資人對高估值成長股的拋售壓力明顯較大。避險行情通常源於宏觀不確定性升溫，例如經濟數據不如預期、地緣政治緊張局勢，或聯準會官員發表鷹派言論，促使資金從風險資產撤離，轉向債券、黃金或現金等相對安全的標的。納斯達克與標普500的跌幅差距擴大，顯示對利率預期最敏感的高本益比科技股，今日面臨最為集中的賣壓。

daily_lesson.explanation_en: Risk-off is a market mood where investors become fearful and pull money out of volatile assets like tech stocks and put it into safer ones like government bonds or cash. You can spot it when growth-heavy indexes like the NASDAQ fall much harder than the broader market. It doesn't mean a crash is coming — it just means traders are being cautious and waiting for clarity before taking new positions.
daily_lesson.explanation_zh: 「避險情緒」（Risk-Off）是指市場上投資人因擔憂前景而將資金從高波動資產（如科技股）撤出，轉移至政府公債或現金等較安全標的的市場氛圍。當納斯達克等成長型指數的跌幅明顯大於大盤時，通常就是避險情緒升溫的訊號。這並不代表市場即將崩盤，而是投資人在局勢明朗前選擇觀望、暫緩進場的謹慎態度。
